name: Return to Cabañeros National Park — from Navas de Estena
name_original: Vuelta al Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — desde Navas de Estena
region_intro: Touring cycling around Navas De Estena is characterized by its location within the Montes de Toledo and proximity to Cabañeros National Park, a region known for its Mediterranean forests and diverse ecosystems. The landscape features ancient holm oak and cork oak woodlands, alongside unique geological formations revealing millions of years of history. The area includes the Estena River, which carves through valleys like Boquerón del Estena, and is defined by Paleozoic quartzite mountain ranges worn by erosion. Best touring cycling routes around Navas De Estena The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — circular by Horcajo de los Montes, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular journey through the diverse landscapes of Cabañeros National Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Return to Cabañeros National Park — from Navas de Estena, a difficult 73.0 miles (117.5 km) path. This long-distance route explores the extensive natural environments of the national park, starting from Navas de Estena. Local touring cyclists also love the Return to Cabañeros National Park via the Torre de Abraham Reservoir, a 47.3 miles (76.2 km) trail leading through the Cabañeros National Park and past the Torre de Abraham Reservoir, often completed in about 5 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Navas De Estena is defined by Mediterranean forests, ancient geological formations, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, including paths of low difficulty. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 9 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Navas De Estena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Navas De Estena?
A: The region is characterized by the ancient Paleozoic quartzite mountain ranges of the Montes de Toledo, which are worn by erosion, creating an undulating landscape. You'll encounter Mediterranean forests dominated by holm and cork oaks, as well as unique geological formations, particularly along the Estena River. While many routes are challenging, some areas like the Boquerón del Estena offer paths of lower difficulty.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are circular options for touring cyclists. One notable route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058383946/parque-nacional-de-cabaneros-circular-by-horcajo-de-los-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — circular by Horcajo de los Montes&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a journey through the diverse landscapes of Cabañeros National Park. Another loop option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menasalbas Village Center – Justice Pillory loop from Hontanar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The area is rich in natural beauty. You can explore geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt; and discover ancient fossils, especially along the Boquerón del Estena. There are also waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Los Navalucillos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5633420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera Chica Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, you might spot &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo&lt;/a&gt;, one of the highest points in the Montes de Toledo.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Navas De Estena?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. In autumn, you might witness the deer rut ('berrea'), while late winter to early spring (February to March) offers opportunities for birdwatching, particularly the return of cranes. Summers can be hot, and winters mild, so planning around these seasons will enhance your experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes starting from Navas De Estena?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186658313/return-to-cabaneros-national-park-from-navas-de-estena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Cabañeros National Park — from Navas de Estena&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route spanning over 117 kilometers. This route allows you to explore the extensive natural environments of the national park directly from the town.

Q: What makes the Boquerón del Estena area special for cyclists?
A: The Boquerón del Estena is considered one of the most beautiful areas in Cabañeros National Park. It offers a journey through landscapes revealing over 500 million years of geological history, with abundant fossils like trilobites. The route largely runs parallel to the Estena River, providing a scenic and generally low-difficulty experience, making it suitable for various cycling abilities.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or significant water bodies?
A: Yes, one route that takes you past a significant water body is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186633221/return-to-cabaneros-national-park-via-the-torre-de-abraham-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Cabañeros National Park via the Torre de Abraham Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. This route leads through the national park and offers views of the Torre de Abraham Reservoir.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Navas De Estena?
A: The touring cycling routes around Navas De Estena are generally considered challenging. All four routes listed in this guide are rated as 'difficult' due to their distances and elevation changes. However, the region also offers paths of lower difficulty, such as those found within the Boquerón del Estena, for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some unique geological features or points of interest for cyclists?
A: The region is a treasure trove for those interested in geology and paleontology. The route through Boquerón del Estena is a designated Geological Interest Site, where you can observe slate formations containing fossils, including trilobites, dating back over 500 million years. Navas de Estena also hosts a permanent exhibition on the Ordovician Period, showcasing local fossils.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Navas De Estena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Navas De Estena are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the immersive experience within the diverse landscapes of Cabañeros National Park and the unique geological features encountered along the way.

Q: Is there public transport access to the Cabañeros National Park area near Navas De Estena?
A: While Navas de Estena is the closest town to Cabañeros National Park, public transport options directly into the park or to specific trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider private transport for reaching starting points of routes within the park. For detailed information on the park, you can refer to &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Caba%C3%B1eros_National_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park on Wikivoyage&lt;/a&gt; or download the official information from &lt;a href="http://www.visitacabaneros.es/InformationinEnglishabouttheCaba%C3%B1erosNationalPark%20.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitacabaneros.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any recreational areas with facilities for cyclists?
A: Yes, the Tabla del Acebo Recreational Area is located near the start of the Boquerón del Estena route. This area provides convenient parking and picnic facilities, along with a 500-meter accessible boardwalk that runs along the Estena River, offering a pleasant spot to rest before or after your ride.
tips: The CM-4017 road crosses Cabañeros National Park between Horcajo de los Montes and Retuerta del Bullaque, offering one of the most scenic routes through the Montes de Toledo. It's ideal for cars, motorcycles, or bicycles, with good road surfaces and spectacular views.

Horcajo has a unique charm: its municipal area is integrated into the Cabañeros National Park. 1 km from the town of Horcajo, is the largest visitor center in the Park, essential to plan a visit. There, a main exhibition shows the different ecosystems of Cabañeros throughout the four seasons of the year. In addition, a quintería in which the culture and traditions of the region are made known. It is completed with projection rooms, a library, a picnic area, parking lots, etc.
